title: Module 1 quiz: The Stripe mental model

questions.prompt: What's the relationship between Products and Prices in Stripe?

choices.text: Product = abstract thing you sell; Price = specific pricing for it. One Product can have many Prices ($20/mo, $200/year, $0.10/unit). Subscriptions reference Prices.

questions.explanation: Products and Prices are separated so one Product can have multiple pricing options. Subscriptions reference Prices, not Products.

questions.prompt: Why use PaymentIntents instead of Charges for new code?

choices.text: PaymentIntents are a state machine handling 3DS / SCA challenges, asynchronous payment methods, off-session retries, and multi-step authorizations natively. Charges are single-shot and don't model these flows cleanly.

questions.explanation: Modern payment flows require state machines (3DS, async, off-session). PaymentIntents handle them; Charges don't. Use PaymentIntents for new code.

questions.prompt: A subscription auto-renewal off-session fails with `requires_action`. Right response?

choices.text: Card needs 3DS auth; user must be present. Surface 'action required' banner / email; complete via Stripe.js on next visit. Don't bypass — issuer requires user-present authentication.

questions.explanation: Off-session 3DS requires the user. Banner + on-session re-confirmation is the pattern.

questions.prompt: A developer copies production env vars (live Stripe keys) into `.env.local`. What happens when they run tests?

choices.text: Tests create real customers and charge real cards. The fix is structural: live keys never in development; env vars only; pre-commit hooks scan for `sk_live_` patterns; clear team documentation.

questions.explanation: Live keys process real charges. Discipline: env-var-only, separate `.env.local` (gitignored), pre-commit scans, team training. Live keys only on production servers.

questions.prompt: Your checkout endpoint crashes after creating a PaymentIntent but before saving the ID. User retries. Without protection?

choices.text: Second PaymentIntent created; user double-charged. Fix: idempotency key per logical operation, stored in DB before the Stripe call, reused on retries. Stripe returns the original intent on the second call.

questions.explanation: Idempotency keys prevent duplicate operations on retries. Generate once, store in DB, reuse on retry. Pair with webhook Event ID dedup for end-to-end safety.
